evidence_excerpt: After a prolonged hospital stay, patient died of respiratory failure and septic shock +128 days following BMT.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: Following day +53, grade IV GI GVHD progressed, required subtotal colectomy, and the patient remained hospitalized until death.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: Presented on day +53 with a 7-day history of severe diarrhea; biopsy showed grade 4 GI GVHD with pneumatosis and likely microperforation; symptoms persisted despite multi-agent treatment.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Posttransplant course was complicated by neutropenic fever and signs of engraftment syndrome with mild tachypnea and generalized rash; responded to a short-term course of prednisone.
He was discharged from hospital on day +20 with no evidence of GvHD, feeling well and eating normally.
He was seen in clinic initially weekly and then every two weeks and he remained well and asymptomatic prior to day +53.
He presented on day +53 after BMT with a 7-day history of severe diarrhea and was admitted to the hospital.
Endoscopy and biopsy revealed grade 4 of 4 GI GvHD with extensive mucosal ulceration, pneumatosis cystoids intestinalis, acute serositis, and likely focal microperforation.
Despite treatment with steroids, calcineurin inhibitors, cellcept, budesonide, and infliximab, the grade IV GI GvHD progressed.
He developed perforation of the cecum requiring subtotal colectomy.
Patient died of respiratory failure and septic shock +128 days following BMT.

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact index time (final treatment plan date) was not explicitly stated; transplant admission was used as the proxy. Long-term quality of life beyond 12 months cannot be assessed as the patient died at approximately 4 months post-transplant. Exact timing of subtotal colectomy within the hospitalization after day +53 was not specified; categorized as short-term based on proximity.
